title: Wire-assistant phase (now phase 2) — Copilot setup steps, other-assistant branch, failure recovery
qaNote: All six ACs verified. AC6 visual gate (Bootstrap with Glean disabled) is story 009's responsibility per outOfScope; data plumbing implemented correctly.

perAc.ac: AC1 — Copilot branch shows exactly two ordered setup steps with chat.useAgentsMdFile in copyable monospace
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx lines 562-579 (Step 1 of 2: enable chat.useAgentsMdFile) and 581-595 (Step 2 of 2: workspace folder open). Setting name rendered as <code> (monospace) at lines 569 and 575. Labels in copilotCheckItems at line 504 also include the setting name. grep -n 'chat.useAgentsMdFile' → hits lines 504, 561, 569, 575. Exactly two steps, ordered 'Step 1 of 2' / 'Step 2 of 2'. PASS.

perAc.ac: AC2 — Verification prompt is verbatim 'Confirm you can read my constitution and context/active.md — tell me my #1 priority.' in a copyable button
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx line 489: const verificationPrompt = 'Confirm you can read my constitution and context/active.md — tell me my #1 priority.'. Node extraction + charCodeAt confirms em-dash is U+2014 matching the AC's \u2014. handleCopy (lines 491-495) calls navigator.clipboard.writeText(verificationPrompt). Button at lines 677-689 triggers handleCopy onClick. grep -n 'Confirm you can read my constitution' → line 489. PASS.

perAc.ac: AC3 — Other-assistant branch displays three panels: (a) Copilot assumption note, (b) CLAUDE.md shim explanation, (c) generic adaptation guidance
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx lines 599-647. Panel (a) lines 601-608: 'The as-is setup assumes GitHub Copilot in VS Code native chat'. Panel (b) lines 610-629: 'CLAUDE.md is a thin shim that points Claude-flavoured tools at AGENTS.md'. Panel (c) lines 631-646: 'Point your assistant at AGENTS.md as its always-loaded instruction file — via a setting, an import directive, or by pasting its contents directly.' Cross-checked against OQ-003.json resolution: 'the reference is GitHub Copilot reading AGENTS.md; Claude Code availability is not assumed' — panel (a) states this explicitly, panel (c) treats Claude Code as one possible non-reference assistant. PASS.

perAc.ac: AC4 — 'That didn't work' toggle shows expandable panel with exact import-debug steps from os-helper SKILL.md Mode 1
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx lines 696-733. Toggle button at line 700, aria-expanded={showDebug} at line 702. Panel conditional on showDebug (line 707). Three content nodes: (1) 'Ask your assistant: What are your standing orders?' (lines 711-715), (2) 'If it cannot describe the constitution, @AGENTS.md did not load.' (lines 716-720), (3) 'Fallback: open AGENTS.md and paste its contents directly into chat.' (lines 721-729). Cross-checked against skills/os-helper/SKILL.md line 91: 'If the assistant doesn't appear to have its standing orders, ask it What are your standing orders? — if it can't describe the constitution, the @AGENTS.md import didn't load. Fallback: open AGENTS.md and paste its contents directly into the chat.' Content matches verbatim intent. PASS.

perAc.ac: AC5 — Navigating away and returning restores assistant-type selection and checkbox states across sessions
perAc.evidence: Answers type (lines 25-28) includes both 'assistant' ('copilot' | 'other') and 'checks' ({installed, contextOpen, testedPaste}). useState initialisation (lines 81-92) reads savedState first (file-backed), then falls back to localStorage, then emptyAnswers. saveOnboarding called in debounced effect (lines 116-124) with answers object including both fields. save-onboarding.ts schema (line 20) uses z.record(z.unknown()) for answers — accepts all subfields. load-onboarding.ts returns the full state. Current onboarding-state.json (cat confirms) has correct shape with 'assistant' and 'checks' nested under answers. PASS.

perAc.ac: AC6 — Next disabled until all three checks ticked; phase 2 lands in completedPhases via story 001's save mechanism
perAc.evidence: Story 005's scope (data plumbing): allWireChecks = Object.values(answers.checks).every(Boolean) (line 136); nextDisabled = phase === 2 && !allWireChecks (line 137). Next button has disabled={nextDisabled} and cursor-not-allowed/opacity-50 classes (lines 215-230). next() calls setCompletedPhases adding current phase before advancing (lines 141-144). Save effect (lines 113-129) depends on [phase, answers, completedPhases] and calls saveOnboarding with completedPhases included. save-onboarding.ts z.array(z.number()) schema (line 21) validates completedPhases. Current onboarding-state.json shows completedPhases: [1] confirming the schema is live and populated. Dev server started successfully on port 3099 (no build errors). Browser automation not available for live click-through, but code path is unambiguous. The visual gate for 'Bootstrap with Glean' in phase 3 is story 009's responsibility; the story outOfScope explicitly lists 'phases 3–5 field changes (stories 003, 009, 010)' and the AC text annotates '(story 009)'. PASS for story 005's scope.

commandsRun: grep -n 'Confirm you can read my constitution' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → PASS — line 489
cd work-hq && npx tsc --noEmit → PASS — no output (zero errors)
cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json → PASS — file exists, completedPhases: [1], checks all false, schema matches save-onboarding validator
node -e (extract verificationPrompt, charCodeAt em-dash) → PASS — U+2014 confirmed, string matches AC verbatim
git diff 6ecb20b~1..6ecb20b | grep secrets → PASS — SECRETS_SCAN_CLEAN
git diff 6ecb20b~1..6ecb20b --name-only | grep migration → PASS — NO_SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS
vite dev --port 3099 (background) → PASS — server ready in 649ms, no build errors
qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan: PASS (no API keys, tokens, passwords, or private keys in diff). Migration check: N/A (no database schema; state is file-backed JSON with no migration runner required).
qaReport.riskNotes: 1. Checkbox state does not reset when switching between Copilot and Other-assistant tabs. If a user ticks all three boxes in Copilot mode then switches to Other, the Next button stays enabled even though they have not verified in the other-assistant context. Not an AC requirement, but could mislead users. 2. navigator.clipboard.writeText is called with void (line 492), swallowing clipboard permission errors silently. On localhost without HTTPS, the Clipboard API may be unavailable in some browser profiles; the user would see no feedback that copying failed. Not an AC requirement.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: 1. Story 009 must implement the 'Bootstrap with Glean' visual disable gate reading completedPhases from onboarding-state.json and showing 'Complete assistant wiring in phase 2 first' when phase 2 is absent — this is the unimplemented half of AC6, pinned to story 009. 2. Consider resetting answers.checks to all-false when the assistant-type selector switches between copilot and other (outside this story's scope). 3. Consider adding a fallback error message when navigator.clipboard.writeText rejects, so users know to copy manually (outside this story's scope).

impl.notes: PhaseWire fully rewritten and repositioned from phase 5 to phase 2. Copilot branch: two ordered steps with chat.useAgentsMdFile in copyable monospace; verification prompt is the exact AC2 string in a click-to-copy button. Other-assistant branch: three panels covering (a) Copilot assumption note, (b) CLAUDE.md shim explanation, (c) generic adaptation guidance. That-didn't-work expandable panel sourced from skills/os-helper/SKILL.md Mode 1 Troubleshooting note. Next button disabled at phase 2 until all three checkboxes are ticked, ensuring completedPhases only records phase 2 after wiring is confirmed (AC6 data plumbing for story 009). AC5 (state persistence) satisfied by existing answers.assistant and answers.checks fields already serialised by story 001 save-onboarding. Story is owned by ui-dev but implemented per explicit user direction.
impl.verificationRun: grep -n 'chat.useAgentsMdFile' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → PASS (lines 497, 555, 563, 569, 574); grep -n 'Confirm you can read my constitution' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → PASS (line 482); npx tsc --noEmit → PASS; npm run format → PASS (no structural changes)

userStory: As a new user configuring my AI assistant, I want step-by-step setup guidance and a copyable verification prompt before I choose a seeding path, so that I can confirm my assistant has loaded my constitution and the bootstrap path is ready to use.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The Copilot branch of phase 2 shows exactly two setup steps in order: (1) enable the VS Code setting `chat.useAgentsMdFile` (setting name displayed as copyable monospace text), and (2) confirm the workspace folder containing AGENTS.md is open in VS Code.

The verification prompt block contains exactly this text: "Confirm you can read my constitution and context/active.md — tell me my #1 priority." — verbatim, in a copyable block that places this string on the clipboard when clicked.

The "Other assistant" branch displays: (a) a note that the as-is setup assumes GitHub Copilot in VS Code native chat, (b) an explanation that CLAUDE.md is a thin shim pointing Claude-flavoured tools at AGENTS.md, and (c) a generic guidance note for adapting the setup to a different assistant.

When the user activates a "That didn't work" toggle or button, an expandable panel appears showing the import-debug steps: ask the assistant "What are your standing orders?" — if it cannot describe the constitution, @AGENTS.md did not load; fallback: open AGENTS.md and paste its contents directly into chat.
Navigating away from phase 2 and returning (including across sessions via story 001's load-on-mount) restores the assistant-type selection (copilot vs other) and all checkbox states.

The "Bootstrap with Glean" option in phase 3 (story 009) is visually disabled and shows an explanatory note — "Complete assistant wiring in phase 2 first" — when `completedPhases` in onboarding-state.json does not include phase 2. `cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json` must show phase 2 in `completedPhases` before the bootstrap option becomes active.

technicalNotes: Edit `PhaseWire` in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`. Phase 2 is now positioned before seeding (ADR-P6-004 amended): the bootstrap path in phase 3 requires a working assistant, so this phase gates it. The verification prompt text is hardcoded (not user-editable). Import-debug steps sourced from the Troubleshooting note in `skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` Mode 1. The `checks` object in `Answers` type must be persisted via story 001's save-onboarding — include `assistant` and `checks` keys in the state shape. Phase 2 completion (all three checkboxes ticked) writes phase 2 into `completedPhases` so story 009 can gate the bootstrap option. No new server functions; UI-only story.
outOfScope: Actual verification of the assistant's response (the wizard cannot read the chat window), phases 3–5 field changes (stories 003, 009, 010), state persistence infrastructure (story 001), the handoff block content (story 006).

verificationPlan.checks: Returns a line number match confirming the exact setting name appears in the phase 2 Copilot branch.

verificationPlan.command: grep -n 'Confirm you can read my constitution' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx
verificationPlan.checks: Returns a line containing the exact verification prompt text — confirms the correct wording is present.

revisionLog.change: Phase renumbered to 2 (wire assistant precedes seeding per amended ADR-P6-004); added AC: phase 2 completion gates bootstrap path in phase 3; phase references updated throughout; userStory updated to reflect pre-seeding position.
revisionLog.reason: ADR-P6-007 requires a working assistant bridge before the bootstrap path is accessible; ADR-P6-004 amended phase order accordingly.
